text: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.
20.1g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
1.6g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin a at 160% daily value stands out most. that is major vitamin support.
potassium at 10% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

on the plus side: carotenoid content gives it extra value. that adds a useful plant-pigment angle beyond the basic carb role most tubers play. fairly filling for the calories. a practical strength for an energy food. easy to use as a wholefood staple. works cleanly in meals without needing much rescue from toppings or processing.

the drawbacks next: naturally sweeter than some tubers. still fine, but it is not the lowest-sugar energy food in the category. healthiness depends on preparation. heavy added fats or sugary toppings can change the practical outcome. protein contribution is light. like most tubers, it is not doing much in the protein lane.
S tier. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
